description:
## What
Run `smallhold compat-test --target=mastodon.social` and fix every failing row in the compat matrix. Required green rows: Follow, Post, Reply, Boost, Like, DM, Media, Edit, Delete. Document any intentional gaps (e.g., features not in scope for v0.1) in `docs/compat.md` with an explicit "— (out of scope v0.1)" notation.

## Why
mastodon.social is the canonical reference implementation and the primary release gate. All protocol shape decisions must match what mastodon.social actually does, not what the spec says.

## Agent team pattern
- Spawn a Research subagent to: run `smallhold compat-test --target=mastodon.social` and capture the output; for each failing row, use curl to capture the exact mastodon.social request/response that fails; identify whether the failure is in outbound format, inbound parsing, or delivery.
- Spawn an Implementation subagent to: fix each failing compatibility issue in the appropriate handler or serialization layer; update `docs/compat.md` with results; ensure no regression on previously passing rows.
- Spawn a Test subagent to: add or update federation replay tests in `tests/federation_replay.rs` for any new mastodon.social AP shapes discovered; verify tests pass with independent DB-state assertions.

## Test discipline
- Independent oracle: live mastodon.social responses captured via curl; compat matrix rows are the oracle (each row is pass/fail, not derived from our own implementation).
- Test approach: `smallhold compat-test` against live mastodon.social for acceptance; fixture replay tests in CI for regression guard.

## Defensive programming checklist
- No `.unwrap()` in handler paths.
- Follow activity: handle `object` as either URI string or full object — both shapes arrive from mastodon.social.
- Delete activity: must 202 silently for unknown post IDs.
- Boost (Announce): `object` may be a URI to an uncached post — fetch and cache before rendering.
- Accept/Reject of Follow must be sent asynchronously via the delivery worker, not inline in the inbox handler.
- Never log full activity bodies at INFO — log type+target+status only.
title: Mastodon 4.x federation compatibility
